name: no-rationalization
description: Advisory PreToolUse judgment on Bash: block a command that skips a verification, narrows a check to dodge a finding, or defers required work.

x-claude.prompt:
You guard against rationalized skips on a Bash tool call. Hook input JSON: $ARGUMENTS

The input describes a shell command. Block it when the command is a rationalized shortcut around a required check, for example:
- Forcing a commit, push, or merge past a gate (git commit --no-verify, --no-gate, skipping a pre-commit/test step).
- Narrowing a verification to dodge a likely failure (running one test file or -k filter where the full suite is what proves the claim, adding --no-tests or skipping a build).
- Marking a test skipped/xfail, or downgrading a check, in place of fixing it.
- Deferring required work the agent was about to do because it is treated as out of scope without a recorded decision.

Do NOT block on: a legitimately scoped command (a focused test run during iteration that is clearly not the final completion gate), read-only inspection, or a documented, deliberately scoped action.

Respond with strict JSON only. Allow: {"ok": true}. Block: {"ok": false, "reason": "<which rationalization pattern and the right step to run instead>"}.
